title: Standardize observedGeneration on sub-reconciler conditions
description:
# GitHub Issue #192: Set observedGeneration consistently on all sub-reconciler conditions

**Category**: refactoring | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Audit all sub-reconcilers and ensure every condition update includes ObservedGeneration set to the current CR's metadata.generation. Currently, setReadyCondition sets it on the aggregate Ready condition (keystone_controller.go:153), but individual sub-reconcilers are inconsistent — some set it (secrets, deployment), others omit it.

**Motivation**: Without observedGeneration on sub-conditions, it is impossible to determine whether a condition reflects the current spec or a previous generation. This makes status-based automation unreliable — a tool checking DatabaseReady=True cannot tell if that status is for the current spec or an older one. Consistent observedGeneration is a Kubernetes API convention.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go`
- `internal/common/conditions/`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Every sub-reconciler condition update includes ObservedGeneration
- [ ] Unit tests verify observedGeneration is set on each condition type
- [ ] No condition in kubectl get keystone -o yaml has a stale observedGeneration after a spec change

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/192

stories.title: Platform automation tool trusts condition observedGeneration
stories.role: platform automation tool (e.g. ArgoCD health check, status controller)
stories.want: every sub-reconciler condition to carry observedGeneration matching the current CR metadata.generation
stories.so_that: I can distinguish whether a condition reflects the current spec or a stale generation and make reliable rollout decisions
criteria: All 52 conditions.SetCondition calls across all sub-reconcilers include ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation
After a spec change increments metadata.generation, every condition's observedGeneration matches the new generation value
No condition type in subConditionTypes can have observedGeneration=0 after at least one reconcile loop
The aggregate Ready condition's observedGeneration matches metadata.generation

stories.title: Operator developer verifies ObservedGeneration via dedicated tests
stories.want: every sub-reconciler test file to have a dedicated ObservedGeneration test function that exercises both True and False condition paths with distinct generation values
stories.so_that: regressions that drop ObservedGeneration from a condition are caught by unit tests, not discovered in production
criteria: Each of the 11 sub-reconciler test files has a TestReconcile*_ConditionObservedGeneration function
Each dedicated test sets generation to a non-default value (not 0 or 1) and asserts the condition matches
Each test covers at least the True and one False condition path with different generation values
All dedicated tests follow the naming convention TestReconcile{SubReconciler}_ConditionObservedGeneration

stories.title: Future contributor does not accidentally omit ObservedGeneration
stories.role: future contributor adding a new sub-reconciler
stories.want: a clear pattern and test convention for ObservedGeneration that I can follow
stories.so_that: new sub-reconcilers are consistent with the existing convention from day one
criteria: Every existing sub-reconciler test file demonstrates the dedicated ObservedGeneration test pattern
The keystone-reconciler reference documentation mentions the ObservedGeneration convention
The integration test verifies all sub-conditions have correct ObservedGeneration after a full reconcile

requirements.description: Every conditions.SetCondition call across all sub-reconcilers SHALL include ObservedGeneration set to keystone.Generation
requirements.rationale: Kubernetes API convention (KEP-1623): observedGeneration allows clients to distinguish whether a condition reflects the current spec or a previous generation. Without it, status-based automation is unreliable.

scenarios.name: Condition set on successful reconcile includes ObservedGeneration
scenarios.when: a sub-reconciler sets a condition with Status=True after successful reconciliation
and_then: the condition is persisted via conditions.SetCondition

scenarios.name: Condition set on in-progress state includes ObservedGeneration
scenarios.when: a sub-reconciler sets a condition with Status=False and a waiting/in-progress reason
and_then: the reconciler requeues with an appropriate delay

scenarios.name: Condition set on error includes ObservedGeneration
scenarios.when: a sub-reconciler sets a condition with Status=False and a failure reason
and_then: the reconciler returns an error for retry

requirements.description: Every sub-reconciler test file SHALL have a dedicated TestReconcile*_ConditionObservedGeneration test function
requirements.rationale: Dedicated test functions make it immediately obvious which sub-reconcilers are covered for ObservedGeneration. Incidental assertions within other test functions are fragile — they can be removed during refactoring without anyone noticing the coverage gap.

scenarios.name: Dedicated test exists for each sub-reconciler
scenarios.then: matches are found for all sub-reconcilers that set conditions: secrets, fernet, credential, database, deployment, bootstrap, policyvalidation, hpa, networkpolicy, healthcheck, trustflush

scenarios.name: Dedicated test uses non-default generation values
scenarios.when: the dedicated test creates a Keystone CR
scenarios.then: it sets Generation to a non-default value (not 0 or 1) such as 7 or 12
and_then: the assertion checks cond.ObservedGeneration equals the explicit generation value

scenarios.name: Dedicated test covers both condition paths
scenarios.when: the sub-reconciler has both True and False condition outcomes
scenarios.then: the dedicated test exercises both paths with different generation values
and_then: each path's condition has the correct ObservedGeneration

requirements.description: Dedicated ObservedGeneration tests SHALL follow the established naming and structure pattern from existing tests
requirements.rationale: Consistency in test naming and structure makes tests discoverable and reviewable. The existing pattern (TestReconcileHPA_ConditionObservedGeneration, TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_ConditionObservedGeneration, etc.) is already established in 5 test files.

scenarios.name: Test function name follows convention
scenarios.when: a new dedicated ObservedGeneration test is added
and_then: the name matches the pattern used by existing dedicated tests

scenarios.name: Test structure follows convention
scenarios.then: it creates a Keystone CR with explicit Generation, runs the sub-reconciler, finds the condition via meta.FindStatusCondition, and asserts ObservedGeneration
and_then: it reuses existing test helpers (e.g. secretsTestKeystone, fernetTestKeystone)

scenarios.name: Test uses the sub-reconciler's existing test scheme and helpers
scenarios.then: it uses the same scheme builder and test helper functions as sibling tests in the same file
and_then: no new test infrastructure is needed

requirements.description: The keystone-reconciler reference documentation SHOULD mention the ObservedGeneration convention for sub-reconciler conditions
requirements.rationale: Documentation of the convention helps future contributors understand the requirement and follow the pattern when adding new sub-reconcilers.

scenarios.name: Documentation mentions ObservedGeneration requirement
scenarios.then: there is a section or note explaining that all sub-reconciler conditions must include ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation
and_then: the documentation references the Kubernetes API convention

scenarios.name: Documentation references the dedicated test pattern
scenarios.when: a developer reads the ObservedGeneration documentation
scenarios.then: it mentions that each sub-reconciler test file should have a dedicated TestReconcile*_ConditionObservedGeneration test

tasks.title: Add TestReconcileSecrets_ConditionObservedGeneration to reconcile_secrets_test.go (CC-0072, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Add a dedicated ObservedGeneration test function to operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets_test.go following the pattern from TestReconcileHPA_ConditionObservedGeneration. Test two paths: (1) SecretStoreNotReady with Generation=7 — mock ClusterSecretStore as not ready, assert SecretsReady=False with ObservedGeneration=7. (2) SecretsAvailable with Generation=12 — provide all secrets ready, assert SecretsReady=True with ObservedGeneration=12. Use existing secretsTestKeystone() and secretsTestScheme() helpers.

tasks.title: Add TestReconcileFernetKeys_ConditionObservedGeneration to reconcile_fernet_test.go (CC-0072, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Add a dedicated ObservedGeneration test function to operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet_test.go. Test two paths: (1) GeneratingKeys with Generation=7 — no existing fernet secret, assert FernetKeysReady=False with ObservedGeneration=7. (2) FernetKeysAvailable with Generation=12 — provide existing secret, assert FernetKeysReady=True with ObservedGeneration=12. Use existing fernetTestKeystone() and fernetTestScheme() helpers.

tasks.title: Add TestReconcileCredentialKeys_ConditionObservedGeneration to reconcile_credential_test.go (CC-0072, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Add a dedicated ObservedGeneration test function to operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential_test.go. Test two paths: (1) GeneratingKeys with Generation=7 — no existing credential secret, assert CredentialKeysReady=False with ObservedGeneration=7. (2) CredentialKeysAvailable with Generation=12 — provide existing secret, assert CredentialKeysReady=True with ObservedGeneration=12. Use existing credentialTestKeystone() and credentialTestScheme() helpers.

tasks.title: Add TestReconcileDatabase_ConditionObservedGeneration to reconcile_database_test.go (CC-0072, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Add a dedicated ObservedGeneration test function to operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database_test.go. Test two paths: (1) ClusterNotReady with Generation=7 — provide MariaDB cluster with Ready=False, assert DatabaseReady=False with ObservedGeneration=7. (2) DatabaseSynced with Generation=12 — provide all prerequisites ready including completed db_sync and schema check jobs, assert DatabaseReady=True with ObservedGeneration=12. Use existing managedKeystone() and dbTestScheme() helpers.

tasks.title: Add TestReconcileDeployment_ConditionObservedGeneration to reconcile_deployment_test.go (CC-0072, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Add a dedicated ObservedGeneration test function to operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go. Test two paths: (1) WaitingForDeployment with Generation=7 — provide not-ready deployment, assert DeploymentReady=False with ObservedGeneration=7. (2) DeploymentReady with Generation=12 — provide ready deployment, assert DeploymentReady=True with ObservedGeneration=12. Use existing deployTestKeystone() and deployTestScheme() helpers.

tasks.title: Add TestReconcileBootstrap_ConditionObservedGeneration to reconcile_bootstrap_test.go (CC-0072, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Add a dedicated ObservedGeneration test function to operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap_test.go. Test three paths: (1) BootstrapInProgress with Generation=5 — provide running job, assert BootstrapReady=False with ObservedGeneration=5. (2) BootstrapComplete with Generation=7 — provide completed job, assert BootstrapReady=True with ObservedGeneration=7. (3) BootstrapFailed with Generation=12 — provide failed job, assert BootstrapReady=False with ObservedGeneration=12. Use existing bootstrapKeystone() and bootstrapTestScheme() helpers.

tasks.title: Update keystone-reconciler.md with ObservedGeneration convention (CC-0072, REQ-004)
tasks.description: Add a subsection to docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md (in the sub-reconciler contract section) documenting the ObservedGeneration convention: (1) Every conditions.SetCondition call must include ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation. (2) Every sub-reconciler test file must have a dedicated TestReconcile*_ConditionObservedGeneration test. (3) Reference the Kubernetes API convention (KEP-1623). Keep it to a short paragraph plus a code snippet showing the pattern.

test_specifications.expected: [unit] Should set ObservedGeneration on CredentialKeysReady=False (GeneratingKeys path, secret not found) and CredentialKeysReady=True (CredentialKeysAvailable path) with distinct generation values

test_specifications.expected: [unit] Should set ObservedGeneration on DatabaseReady=False (ClusterNotReady path) and DatabaseReady=True (DatabaseSynced path) with distinct generation values

test_specifications.expected: [unit] Should set ObservedGeneration on DeploymentReady=False (WaitingForDeployment path) and DeploymentReady=True (DeploymentReady path) with distinct generation values

test_specifications.story: Operator developer verifies ObservedGeneration via dedicated tests
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Should set ObservedGeneration on BootstrapReady=False (BootstrapFailed and BootstrapInProgress paths) and BootstrapReady=True (BootstrapComplete path) with distinct generation values

test_specifications.story: Platform automation tool trusts condition observedGeneration
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When ClusterSecretStore is not ready and Generation=7, SecretsReady condition has ObservedGeneration=7

test_specifications.story: Platform automation tool trusts condition observedGeneration
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When MariaDB cluster is not ready and Generation=7, DatabaseReady condition has ObservedGeneration=7

test_specifications.story: Platform automation tool trusts condition observedGeneration
test_specifications.expected: [integration] After a full reconcile loop, all conditions in status have ObservedGeneration matching the CR's metadata.generation — already exists and passes

test_specifications.story: Future contributor does not accidentally omit ObservedGeneration
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Test function is named TestReconcileSecrets_ConditionObservedGeneration (matching convention), uses secretsTestKeystone() and secretsTestScheme() helpers (no new infrastructure), and finds conditions via meta.FindStatusCondition

test_specifications.story: Future contributor does not accidentally omit ObservedGeneration
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Test function is named TestReconcileBootstrap_ConditionObservedGeneration (matching convention), reuses bootstrapKeystone() and bootstrapTestScheme() helpers, and covers all 3 condition paths (Failed, InProgress, Complete) with distinct generation values

test_specifications.story: Future contributor does not accidentally omit ObservedGeneration
test_specifications.expected: Documentation section describes ObservedGeneration convention and dedicated test pattern for sub-reconciler conditions

test_specifications.test_function: N/A (documentation — code snippet and test naming reference)
test_specifications.story: Future contributor does not accidentally omit ObservedGeneration
test_specifications.expected: Documentation includes a code snippet showing ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation in a condition and mentions the TestReconcile*_ConditionObservedGeneration naming convention

review_criteria: All 11 sub-reconciler test files have a dedicated TestReconcile*_ConditionObservedGeneration test function (grep for 'func TestReconcile.*_ConditionObservedGeneration' yields 11 matches)
Each dedicated test uses non-default generation values (not 0 or 1) and asserts cond.ObservedGeneration equals the explicit value
Each dedicated test covers both True and False condition paths (or all relevant paths for sub-reconcilers with 3+ conditions)
All tests pass: `make test` in operators/keystone/ completes with 0 failures
No production code changes: only *_test.go and docs/ files are modified
Tests follow the established pattern from TestReconcileHPA_ConditionObservedGeneration: same structure, naming, helper reuse
Documentation in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md mentions the ObservedGeneration convention and dedicated test pattern
golangci-lint passes: no lint errors in modified test files
implementation_notes:
**Key finding from codebase audit:** All 52 conditions.SetCondition calls across all 12 sub-reconciler files already include ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation. The implementation described in issue #192 is complete — no production code changes are needed.

**What remains:** 6 of 11 sub-reconciler test files lack dedicated TestReconcile*_ConditionObservedGeneration test functions. The 5 that already have them (HPA, NetworkPolicy, TrustFlush, HealthCheck, PolicyValidation) were added as part of their respective feature implementations. The 6 missing (Secrets, Fernet, Credential, Database, Deployment, Bootstrap) predate the convention and only have incidental ObservedGeneration assertions within other test functions.

**Pattern to follow:** See TestReconcileHPA_ConditionObservedGeneration in reconcile_hpa_test.go:110 as the canonical example. Structure: (1) create Keystone CR via existing helper, (2) set Generation to a non-default value, (3) set up prerequisites for the desired condition path, (4) run the sub-reconciler, (5) find the condition via meta.FindStatusCondition, (6) assert ObservedGeneration equals the set generation. Repeat for alternative paths with different generation values.

**reconcile_config.go is NOT affected:** This sub-reconciler does not set any conditions — it returns a ConfigMap name. It is listed in the issue's affected areas but requires no action.

**Existing integration coverage:** TestIntegration_ObservedGeneration in integration_test.go:624 already verifies that after a full reconcile loop, all conditions have ObservedGeneration matching the CR's metadata.generation. The dedicated unit tests add per-sub-reconciler regression coverage.

**No centralized safeguard needed:** The conditions.SetCondition wrapper delegates to meta.SetStatusCondition which accepts ObservedGeneration but cannot enforce it at compile time (Go struct literal with named fields allows zero-value omission). The dedicated tests serve as the enforcement mechanism.
